title: Kurinjimala Sanctuary
tagline: Kurinjimala Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Kurinjimala Sanctuary protects the approximately 32 hectare core habitat of the endangered Neelakurinji plant in the Kottakamboor and Vattavada villages in Devikulam Taluk, in the
overview.description: Kurinjimala Sanctuary protects the approximately 32 hectare core habitat of the endangered Neelakurinji plant in the Kottakamboor and Vattavada villages in Devikulam Taluk, in the Idukki district of Kerala, a state in southern India.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kottakamboor
topPlaces.description: Kottakamboor is a village in Devikulam taluk of Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India.

topPlaces.name: Vattavada
topPlaces.description: Vattavada is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, bordering Tamil Nadu, India. The village is located along Palani Hills in Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Mannavanur
topPlaces.description: Mannavanur (Mannavanor, Mannavannur), is a farming village of 1437 households in Mannavanor Panchayat, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India, 35 kilometres (22 mi) west from Kodaikanal It is 7 kilometres (4 mi) by road from Kumbur village and 9 kilometres (6 mi) from Kilanavayal village. Altitude is 1,880 metres (6,170 ft).

topPlaces.name: Keezhanthoor
topPlaces.description: Keezhanthoor is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India. The village is in the Western Ghats and is known for growing apples.

topPlaces.name: Sheep Research Center (Mannavanur)
topPlaces.description: Southern Regional Research Centre (SRRC) is a regional centre of the Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute (CSWRI), a premier Institution of the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (I.C.A.R), New Delhi. The SRRC was founded on 16 November 1965 and has been active for over 45 years at Mannavanur.

topPlaces.name: Vandaravu
topPlaces.description: Vandaravu (Tamil: வண்டறாவு) is the highest peak in the Palani hills (Western Ghats) in Tamil Nadu, India. It shares border between Devikulam taluk in Idukki district and Kodaikanal taluk in Dindigul district.

topPlaces.name: Manjampatti Valley
topPlaces.description: Manjampatti Valley is a 110.9 km2 (42.8 sq mi) protected area in the eastern end of Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park (IGWS&NP) in Tirupur District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is a pristine drainage basin of shola and montane rainforest with high biodiversity recently threatened by illegal land clearing and cultivation.

topPlaces.name: Pampadum Shola National Park
topPlaces.description: Pampadum Shola National Park is the smallest national park in Idukki district of Kerala in India. It is on the border with Kodaikanal, Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu.

items.note: Kottakamboor is a village in Devikulam taluk of Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India.
Vattavada is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, bordering Tamil Nadu, India. The village is located along Palani Hills in Western Ghats.

Mannavanur (Mannavanor, Mannavannur), is a farming village of 1437 households in Mannavanor Panchayat, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India, 35 kilometres (22 mi) west from Kodaikanal It is 7 kilometres (4 mi) by road from Kumbur village and 9 kilometres (6 mi) from Kilanavayal village. Altitude is 1,880 metres (6,170 ft).
Keezhanthoor is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India. The village is in the Western Ghats and is known for growing apples.

Southern Regional Research Centre (SRRC) is a regional centre of the Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute (CSWRI), a premier Institution of the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (I.C.A.R), New Delhi. The SRRC was founded on 16 November 1965 and has been active for over 45 years at Mannavanur.

Vandaravu (Tamil: வண்டறாவு) is the highest peak in the Palani hills (Western Ghats) in Tamil Nadu, India. It shares border between Devikulam taluk in Idukki district and Kodaikanal taluk in Dindigul district.

Manjampatti Valley is a 110.9 km2 (42.8 sq mi) protected area in the eastern end of Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park (IGWS&NP) in Tirupur District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is a pristine drainage basin of shola and montane rainforest with high biodiversity recently threatened by illegal land clearing and cultivation.
Pampadum Shola National Park is the smallest national park in Idukki district of Kerala in India. It is on the border with Kodaikanal, Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kurinjimala Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kurinjimala Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kurinjimala Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (92 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (92 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kurinjimala Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kurinjimala Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kurinjimala Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kurinjimala Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kurinjimala Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kottakamboor, Vattavada, Mannavanur, Keezhanthoor, Sheep Research Center (Mannavanur).
